Supervisory arrangements: The Individual Consultant shall perform the tasks under the overall authority of the Regional Director of the UNESCO Regional Office in Bangkok and Office for UN Coordination in Asia and the Pacific, and the direct supervision of the Head of the UNESCO Project Antenna Office in Yangon, working closely with the UNESCO Education Team. The Individual consultant’s responsibilities include the following: 3.1. Assist in the analysis of household survey datasets, including data organization, data analysis, and preparation of statistical tables and visualizations. 3.2. Conduct desk reviews and background research on Myanmar’s education sector, including education access, participation, continuity, equity, vulnerability, service delivery, and crisis- related barriers to learning. This includes the identification, synthesis, and summarization of relevant literature, policy documents, and research findings. 3.3. Support the conceptualization and development of analytical frameworks for education assessments and analysis to be conducted by UNESCO, including indicator mapping, data source mapping, methodological documentation, and preparation of technical background materials. 3.4. Contribute to the preparation of research reports, presentations, briefing notes, and other knowledge products arising from the analysis and survey activities. 3.5. Assist in data documentation, quality assurance, and coordination with project team members and partners as required.

Mercy Corps is seeking a service provider or experienced national consultant to conduct a rigorous Gender Equality, Disability and Social Inclusion (GEDSI) analysis to contribute to achieving gender-transformative outcomes for the WAVES Program in the Ayeyarwady Delta, Myanmar. The analysis will map differentiated needs, risks, and power dynamics across target groups — including women, girls, persons with disabilities, youth, ethnic minorities, and landless households — examining gender roles, social norms, and exclusion mechanisms that shape access to natural resources, livelihoods, and decision-making. The consultant will assess barriers and enablers to equitable participation in coastal resource governance and value chains, identify GEDSI risks across all WAVES outputs, and generate evidence-based recommendations to mainstream GEDSI across the full project cycle. Key outputs include a comprehensive GEDSI Action Plan aligned with the WAVES logframe, Mercy Corps’ GESI Integration Toolkit and OCEAN GEDSI Empowering standards, and a participatory Sensemaking Workshop to validate findings and co-design priority actions — building local ownership of gender-transformative programming from project inception.
